label: Period 1
description: The first period contains only hydrogen and helium -- the two lightest elements, formed in the Big Bang. They fill the 1s orbital.

label: Period 2
description: Period 2 fills the 2s and 2p orbitals, spanning lithium to neon. It introduces the first nonmetals, the smallest halogens, and the lightest noble gas after helium.

label: Period 3
description: Period 3 fills the 3s and 3p orbitals, from sodium to argon. It mirrors period 2 in structure but the elements are larger and more metallic.

label: Period 4
description: Period 4 is the first to include d-block elements, expanding from 8 to 18 elements. It runs from potassium to krypton and introduces the first row of transition metals.

label: Period 5
description: Period 5 mirrors period 4 with a second row of transition metals, from rubidium to xenon. It includes technetium, the lightest element with no stable isotopes.

label: Period 6
description: Period 6 expands to 32 elements by introducing the f-block lanthanides. It spans caesium to radon and contains some of the densest and rarest stable elements.

label: Period 7
description: Period 7 includes the actinides and all known synthetic superheavy elements, from francium to oganesson. Many of its members are radioactive and exist only briefly in laboratories.
